230136.explanation: We want the municipality’s finances to remain healthy. We use property tax (OZB) to pay for important provisions, such as livability, maintenance, and safety. If we lower the OZB, we must cut elsewhere, which directly affects residents. Therefore, we do not lower the OZB lightly.

230137.explanation: We understand that the dog tax sometimes feels unfair. But we use this money fully for facilities for dogs and owners, such as waste bins and enforcement. If we abolish the tax, a shortfall arises that must be supplemented by cuts or other taxes.

230138.explanation: We support entrepreneurs but want to maintain Heumen’s green and open character. That is why we first focus on smarter use of existing sites. Expansion only occurs if truly necessary and fitting with the landscape. This way, we keep the villages livable.

230140.explanation: This is still legally regulated. However, we also want to help starters, families, and the elderly. We choose a balanced distribution of homes and build affordable houses so everyone has equal chances of suitable housing.

230141.explanation: We want residents to be able to dispose of bulky waste clearly and affordably. We do not support the proposal to make the first 200 kilos free. Making it free involves high costs, which we must cover elsewhere. Recycling waste is important.

230146.explanation: We want to limit high-rise buildings as much as possible. They do not fit the village and green character of our municipality. Moreover, existing homes may suffer disadvantages, such as shadowing and less privacy. Only limited high-rise in the Kanaalzone can be appropriate.

230148.explanation: More parking spaces often mean less greenery and more hard surfaces. We first want better use of existing spots, for example by setting time limits or better signage. Only if that is insufficient will we look further.

230149.explanation: We consider a balanced male-female distribution important because it makes governance stronger and more recognizable. We strive for 50/50, but quality and availability also matter. If it temporarily does not work, we explain this clearly.

230150.explanation: We find it important that residents think along, but referenda are expensive and often reach few people. We choose low-threshold methods such as meetings, online surveys, and conversations in villages. This way we involve more residents simply and affordably.

230153.explanation: We give farmers space for appropriate side activities, but not everything fits everywhere. We want to protect the landscape and prevent messy growth. That is why we work with clear rules and customization, so developments fit the farmyard and surroundings.

230154.explanation: Local energy projects create support and let residents share in proceeds. Therefore, we support cooperatives and neighborhood initiatives with knowledge and sometimes financial help. Together, we sustainably develop our villages in a fair way.

230161.explanation: A fireworks show costs a lot of money and does not automatically solve private nuisance. We prefer to invest in safety, prevention, and activities for youth. This way, New Year's remains festive and responsible. Moreover, a ban already applies.

230163.explanation: It is primarily the entrepreneur’s responsibility. In cases where costs are disproportionately high compared to benefits, we must provide tailored solutions.

230164.explanation: Scattered individual homes in rural areas give a messy appearance and harm the landscape. We choose small clusters and clear rules so new homes fit the environment. Therefore, we do not allow arbitrary building on agricultural land.

230167.explanation: We find it important that every child has the chance to learn music. Music stimulates creativity and provides fun and self-confidence. Therefore, we want to continue supporting music lessons with subsidies so that costs are not a barrier for parents.

230168.explanation: Associations are important for meeting and health. They remain responsible for their own finances, but for large, necessary investments the municipality can help. This keeps our villages active and vital.

230172.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent dumping and nuisance but must be used targeted and temporarily. We do not choose cameras everywhere but wisely deploy them where they really work.

230173.explanation: Everyone must be able to live safely. That is why we want regional cooperation for good shelter for people who are unsafe. A facility in Heumen can be part of that solution but must be organized professionally and carefully.

230174.explanation: We want to build for our own residents. Therefore, we want people from Heumen to have priority for social housing, as long as legally allowed. This keeps villages livable and strengthens cohesion.

230178.explanation: We help people with customized solutions so they can participate appropriately. Mandatory work makes little difference and often causes stress. We choose guidance, work-learning trajectories, and support that truly helps.

230180.explanation: We are not immediately in favor of raising the income limit because that never solves all problems. Even with a higher limit, some people always fall just outside the scheme. Therefore, we find customized solutions very important. We want to help these residents specifically when needed.

230182.explanation: More and more people drive electric. Therefore, we want sufficient charging points, well distributed in the villages and without causing excessive parking pressure. This helps residents switch to sustainable mobility.

230183.explanation: Slower driving makes streets safer, calmer, and cleaner. That is why we want more 30-kilometer zones at logical locations, such as school routes and village streets.

230184.explanation: Care must fit a person’s background and feelings. Therefore, we believe residents should be allowed to choose a care provider that suits them, as long as this is within the rules. This keeps care personal and effective.

230186.explanation: We want enough homes for starters and people who need a house quickly. Therefore, we do not focus on flexible housing but on quickly realizable homes that fit our villages. These homes can be placed quickly and help relieve pressure.

230187.explanation: Events provide fun, meetings, and connection. They are important for our villages. Therefore, we support organizers with space, subsidies, and help.

230188.explanation: We have chosen one shelter location in the municipality. Dispersion over multiple villages proved undesirable and caused unrest. With one central place, we keep shelter manageable, well supervised, and fitting our villages.

230136.explanation: With these revenues, the municipality can maintain part of the services for residents.

230137.explanation: A purpose tax is acceptable in itself but should never exceed covering costs.

230138.explanation: Yes, but not for traditional industry; we gladly facilitate small-scale work locations for circular entrepreneurs.

230140.explanation: Eens, en dit geldt voor alle kwetsbare mensen met een urgente woonbehoefte in onze gemeente. De woningcorporatie moet daarom het aandeel sociale huurwoningen zo snel mogelijk vergroten.

230141.explanation: 'Gratis afval' bestaat niet; voor de verwerking of verbranding moet worden betaald, dus laat de vervuiler ervoor betalen.

230146.explanation: Om voldoende (betaalbare) woningen te kunnen bouwen zonder belangrijke groengebieden op te offeren, zal op bepaalde plekken - zoals in project Kanaalzone - hoge woonflats worden gebouwd.

230148.explanation: We willen juist parkeerplaatsen, versteend gebied en blik in het centrum, inruilen voor groene verblijfs- en ontmoetingsruimten.

230149.explanation: We hechten aan een goede afspiegeling van de Heumense bevolking, maar ook aan de vrijheid van politieke partijen om hun eigen wethouders-kandidaten te kiezen; het draait uiteindelijk vooral om de geschiktheid van kandidaten.

230150.explanation: Wij vinden goede communicatie met en het creëren van draagvlak onder inwoners erg belangrijk. Bij belangrijke onderwerpen is een referendum een manier om inwoners direct invloed te geven op de politieke besluitvorming.

230153.explanation: Niet altijd; Het geldt alleen als het ondernemers ondersteunt in een duurzame (landbouw)onderneming.

230154.explanation: De financiële steun moet vooral bestaan uit begeleiding van inwoners, die een dergelijk duurzaam initiatief nemen. De realisatie zelf kan door middel van crowdfunding plaatsvinden. Eigendom en beheer blijft dan in handen van inwoners.

230161.explanation: Een dergelijk evenement leidt tot ontmoeting en verbroedering en voorkomt het (illegaal) individueel afsteken van vuurwerk, met alle veiligheidsrisico's van dien.

230163.explanation: Een subsidie kan een ondernemer aanmoedigen er werk van te maken en hierin zijn maatschappelijke verantwoordelijkheid te nemen.

230164.explanation: Het leidt tot verstening van het buitengebied en een afname van areaal voor andere groene functies zoals voedselbos en natuurinclusieve landbouw.

230167.explanation: Elk basisschoolkind zou muziekles moeten kunnen krijgen. Als gezinnen dat niet (helemaal) zelf kunnen betalen, zou dat gesubsidieerd kunnen worden.

230168.explanation: Sporten is gezond en draagt in verenigingsverband bij aan sociale interactie. Een financiële bijdrage kan verenigingen door financieel moeilijke tijden helpen.

230172.explanation: Op bepaalde plekken kan cameratoezicht een goede aanvulling zijn om buurten en wijken leefbaar en veilig te houden. Het aantal zal, gelet op de privacy, wel tot een minimum beperkt moeten worden.

230173.explanation: Ook in onze gemeente moeten we vrouwen op kunnen vangen als dat nodig is. De exacte locatie bepaal je samen met buurgemeenten in regionaal verband.

230174.explanation: Zolang er niet veel meer betaalbare huur- en koopwoningen zijn bijgebouwd, zijn sociale huurwoningen heel schaars en mogen ze met voorrang worden toegewezen aan inwoners in kwetsbare posities en met een urgente woningbehoefte uit onze gemeente.

230178.explanation: We willen mensen niet verplichten, maar het aanbieden van maatschappelijk zinvol werk kan hun kansen op arbeidsparticipatie in sommige gevallen wel vergroten.

230180.explanation: We verhogen de individuele bijzondere bijstand tot 130% van het sociaal minimum en verruimen de meedoen-regelingen, zoals het Heumens Tegoed. Ook willen we een betere bekendheid van de ondersteuningsmogelijkheden.

230182.explanation: In buurten en straten, waar een privé-aansluiting niet mogelijk is, faciliteren we aanvragen voor (extra) openbare laadpalen.

230183.explanation: Het is goed als in de dorpen en wijken de snelheid omlaag gaat. De doorgaande wegen zoals de Rijksweg vragen om andere (herinrichtings)oplossingen om een lagere snelheid af te dwingen.

230184.explanation: Een passend en adequaat zorgaanbod is leidend. We zijn daarnaast voorstander van vrije keuze, maar het is vaak onhaalbaar om voor elke geloofsovertuiging aanpassingen te maken.

230186.explanation: Een mooie, duurzame woning in een groene en gezonde omgeving waar je over vijftig jaar nog steeds wilt wonen, levert meer op dan een tijdelijke flexwoning. Wij staan wel open voor initiatieven vanuit de samenleving in goed overleg met omwonenden.

230187.explanation: Evenementen zijn mooi voor ontmoeting en sociale cohesie en dat faciliteren we graag, maar niet zozeer met geld.

230188.explanation: Spreiding over de dorpen in kleinere locaties heeft onze voorkeur. In de afgelopen periode is echter een besluit genomen voor één locatie, omdat spreiding niet haalbaar bleek om goede opvang te realiseren.

230136.explanation: Beter geformuleerd, mag niet verder stijgen dan de jaarlijkse indexatie. De OZB in onze gemeente Heumen was heel lang, heel hoog (na Nijmegen zo ongeveer het hoogste in de regio) en nu op een acceptabeler niveau gekomen, vergelijkbaar met de buurgemeenten.

230138.explanation: Heumen heeft beperkt ruimte voor bedrijven, regionaal wordt er geconcentreerd aanbod gecreëerd, voor zover nodig.

230140.explanation: Aansluiten op landelijke bepalingen en dat is dat urgentie bepalend moet zijn, niet afkomst of status.

230141.explanation: Betaalbaar en uitvoerbaar houden, geen blanco cheque

230148.explanation: Bereikbaarheid is randvoorwaarde voor leefbaarheid, overwegen verdiepte parkeergarage op Zuid-Plein (heeft de VVD al eens eerder voorgesteld, daar knapt het centrum een stuk van op)

230149.explanation: Kwaliteit en competentie gaan voor quota.

230150.explanation: Referenda suggereren zeggenschap die niet gekozenen niet hebben. Het maakt besluitvorming onduidelijk en inwoners uiteindelijk ontevreden. Dit is niet het systeem waarvoor we in Nederland hebben gekozen.

230161.explanation: Tegelijkertijd handhavend optreden tegen vuurwerk door particulieren.

230164.explanation: Bouwen is enkel toegestaan binnen de bestaande contouren. Zo staat het in de strategische visie, om de kwaliteit van het buitengebied te behouden.

230167.explanation: Is verantwoordelijkheid van ouders. Wel bijspringen bij gezinnen met een laag inkomen.

230172.explanation: Use proportionally in public spaces and access roads, town centers, and neighborhoods.

230178.explanation: Participate where possible. This is already existing legislation and policy.

230180.explanation: Do not remove financial incentives that encourage people to work. Poverty in the Netherlands has more than halved in recent years, mainly due to national policy. This does not require supplementation by the municipality of Heumen.

230183.explanation: Evaluate per situation. For example, in residential neighborhoods yes, on through roads no.

230184.explanation: Provided that the additional costs for that free choice are charged to the person receiving care and support. The municipality is not a store with free choice, and residents with help requests are not customers.

230136.explanation: Property tax makes an important contribution to municipal finances. We do not want to increase the burden on our residents beyond inflation and do not shift our financial responsibilities to the future.

230137.explanation: The money should be spent on dog-related costs.

230138.explanation: Our municipality has several business parks with numerous companies and a varied range of goods and services. We see no reason for expansion, but we do commit to the optimal use of the existing business parks and plots.

230140.explanation: CDA Heumen wants to provide status holders with a place, but no priority in social housing. The housing shortage requires a fair distribution for everyone.

230141.explanation: Free disposal for polluters means higher costs for residents in general, because the processing costs for the municipality and environmental site taxes will increase.

230146.explanation: Higher housing development in Malden is possible, as long as careful attention is paid to integration, livability, and the scale of the environment. The Canal zone will partly become high-rise, and this was established in the past council period.

230148.explanation: We see no major parking problems around the center of Malden on normal days and therefore no need for more parking spaces.

230149.explanation: We value everyone's qualities and capacities to contribute to the council.

230150.explanation: Everyone should have opportunities to influence policy, and we appreciate residents' ideas. However, we do not think a referendum is the best way to achieve this and therefore choose other forms of citizen participation.

230153.explanation: We want to cooperate in small-scale tourism initiatives and small-scale economic diversification or innovation in the countryside.

230154.explanation: This form of subsidy already exists for individual residents. It should also be accessible to cooperating residents.

230161.explanation: A municipal fireworks show has disadvantages: environmental pollution, unclear costs, and not everyone enjoys it. If something is organized, it must be safe, affordable, and supported by residents.

230163.explanation: We strongly support accessible public spaces and also want to encourage entrepreneurs to make their businesses more accessible. This can be financially supported through existing schemes, whereby the municipality can contribute to information provision.

230164.explanation: This is already possible under the municipality's 'red-for-red' scheme. We want to expand this scheme to make it possible to build several small homes near the villages.

230167.explanation: Getting acquainted with music is important for every child, but interests can be broader than just music lessons, for example sports or other activities. If the municipality invests in this, we prefer it to be through schools.

230168.explanation: Sports clubs are important because they contribute to a healthy lifestyle and bringing people together. If a vital function such as community building is at risk, financial contributions can be considered.

230172.explanation: Camera surveillance should only be in necessary locations, and restraint is required. Respect for privacy is also important.

230173.explanation: Heumen is a smaller municipality; therefore, facilities like this are arranged in partnerships with regional partners and national networks. We ensure that assistance remains of good quality and financially affordable.

230174.explanation: A good balance is important, with part of the houses allocated to home seekers from our own municipality.

230178.explanation: In principle, we encourage people with social assistance benefits to contribute to society where possible, for example through informal care, volunteer work, or a trajectory leading to paid work. This depends on possibilities; customization is important.

230180.explanation: Poverty is hard to translate into an income threshold and mainly depends on opportunities people experience. We want to maintain the existing income threshold and also guard against a large poverty trap for those just above the threshold.

230182.explanation: Residents can request a charging spot from the municipality. The location will then better match the resident's wishes and be appropriate. In general, we do not see the need to create more charging spots.

230183.explanation: This speed limit has already been implemented in various traffic situations but remains a focus point for provincial roads running through the villages. We support specific solutions for places where safety is at risk.

230184.explanation: Care providers are trained to consider diversity and are, in most cases, fully capable of providing care. Often choice is simply not possible, and this will put pressure on access to care.

230186.explanation: As long as it is necessary, we are open to temporary housing. Hopefully, the demand will decrease with the realization of housing projects. Temporary must truly be temporary.

230187.explanation: We welcome initiatives that lead to community building. Initiatives should certainly be supported. Helping citizens to ultimately do it themselves would be a good guideline. For example, simplifying rules and assisting with permit applications.

230188.explanation: We still prefer that asylum seekers are distributed over the 4 villages. Practice has shown that distribution over the villages was not feasible. Safety and livability remain very important principles-for both residents and asylum seekers.

230136.explanation: Heumen has many facilities for its size. To afford these, we should not lower the property tax. The property tax is aligned with surrounding municipalities.

230137.explanation: The dog tax in Heumen is used for maintaining dog walking areas and has already been reduced.

230138.explanation: They are necessary but depend on the available space.

230140.explanation: Priority but proportionate. So, this depends on the number of available homes in relation to the number of home seekers.

230141.explanation: This provision is currently cost-neutral. If we make it partly free, the municipality will incur a loss.

230146.explanation: If we really want to build affordable housing for the region, residential towers are necessary. (Limited high) residential towers will be built in the Canal Zone.

230148.explanation: We encourage cycling, and more parking spaces work against that.

230149.explanation: Depends on the qualities of the members of the executive board.

230150.explanation: In exceptional situations, residents should have the right to request a referendum that results in a substantial advisory opinion for the municipal council.

230153.explanation: Completely dependent on the local situation.

230154.explanation: Generally no, but in specific situations it must be possible.

230161.explanation: That can be done if there is a budget and expert staff available. It should not be mandatory.

230163.explanation: This is the responsibility of the entrepreneurs.

230164.explanation: That depends on whether the farm is still operational. Cleaning up stables and building a house instead should be possible.

230167.explanation: Only for those on minimum income.

230168.explanation: In principle, that is the responsibility of the members.

230172.explanation: Only in public spaces where there is a nuisance and in consultation with the local residents.

230173.explanation: As far as known, there is insufficient demand for this in Heumen.

230174.explanation: In Heumen housing is also built for the region, but part of it gives priority to residents of Heumen.

230178.explanation: The question is, of course, why a social assistance benefit is being received and what kind of work it concerns.

230180.explanation: There are now sufficient resources available for people on minimum income.

230182.explanation: To achieve the climate goals, it is necessary that residents have more opportunities to charge their electric cars.

230184.explanation: That cannot be guaranteed. Striving for it is fine.

230186.explanation: A large housing shortage can be slightly reduced by temporary housing.

230187.explanation: In principle, that is the responsibility of the organizers.

230188.explanation: For integration, spreading is better but financially not feasible.

230136.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is an important source of income for the municipality, alongside funding from the national government and fees. The national government has announced it wants to contribute less. It is therefore a utopia to lower property tax. Through good financial policy, keep property tax as low as possible.

230137.explanation: Under the motto 'the polluter pays,' we do not want to abolish dog tax, but to reduce it to the level of the costs made for maintaining dog areas. These areas must first be qualitatively improved throughout the municipality.

230138.explanation: Suitable business parks form the basis for good entrepreneurship. We will look at whether these can be located more outside the centers so that inner locations can be converted into residential areas. Also consider using business parks for sustainability.

230140.explanation: Status holders do not receive automatic priority; social rental housing is fairly distributed, while the municipality fulfills its legal duty with temporary or alternative housing without extra pressure on the housing market. Residents who have waited a long time take precedence.

230141.explanation: Collection must be low-threshold precisely to ensure it does not end up in the environment and can be recycled.

230146.explanation: High residential towers usually do not fit the scale of Malden, unless they are of limited height, carefully integrated, and clearly contribute to local housing needs.

230148.explanation: Currently, parking pressure in the center is usually manageable, especially outside peak times. Additional parking spaces would also come at too great a cost to greenery, space, and livability.

230149.explanation: Appointments based on expertise are already challenging enough. Do not create more hurdles. Moreover, positive discrimination is also just discrimination.

230150.explanation: Being heard based on arguments is the most important thing in politics. Citizens must be part of the discussion.

230153.explanation: Farmers and other entrepreneurs must be facilitated in such plans.

230154.explanation: Support in the form of expertise (to guide the process) and providing certain facilities is okay, but not unconditional budget support.

230161.explanation: An alternative would be to have neighborhood committees organize this at several locations and make them responsible for keeping order and preventing accidents. Organized is better than chaos.

230163.explanation: The municipality must set requirements for accessibility in its location policies and it is fine to offer financial incentives for this. However, the majority of the investment must come from the entrepreneur.

230164.explanation: There is a housing shortage; therefore, this is a good opportunity for additional housing construction. However, we believe these homes should only be used for personal use, i.e., for one's own parents or children.

230167.explanation: Music education is important for development. To the extent parents cannot pay for it themselves, in the opinion of De Kiezersclub, it can be arranged via the Heumen Credit or via Stichting Leergeld, not through direct subsidies. With the restriction that only one music lesson can be followed at a time.

230168.explanation: The Voters' Club wants to help (sports) associations as much as possible with making their association buildings more sustainable. The financing and operation will primarily be the responsibility of the associations themselves.

230172.explanation: Camera surveillance can be effective, but it is not a priority. The Voters' Club wants to use this tool sparingly in public spaces, only in locations where social safety is at risk. Video footage may only be stored for a short period of time.

230173.explanation: If it appears necessary here, building such a center should be considered. For now, it is more logical to concentrate such centers in the larger municipalities and make agreements accordingly.

230174.explanation: There is a shortage of housing in Heumen for our own children. Therefore, where possible, rental homes should first be offered to residents of our own municipality.

230178.explanation: See this as “participation” from a social perspective.

230180.explanation: Income limits are only part of the assessment. Support should also be available to people who encounter problems even above the income thresholds. Note: Support can also be offered in the form of advice or loans!

230182.explanation: However, these spaces should primarily be located outside the parking lots around the shopping centers. Currently, parking spaces in the center often stand empty, while there is a supply of cars. Of course, there must still be enough spaces for the residents of the center.

230183.explanation: A 30 km/h zone everywhere within the built-up area enhances safety and reduces fine particle emissions. In Malden, fine particulate matter limits have been exceeded for years. It is possible that non-local traffic will then choose the A73.

230184.explanation: Care should be based on what is needed, not on what people would prefer.

230186.explanation: Heumen must build and renovate with a view to the longer lifespan of the homes.

230187.explanation: Events strengthen the cohesion of residents and the identity of the municipality. It is important, however, that these events are of good quality and accessible to everyone.

230188.explanation: It is a distribution law. The Voters' Club advocates the best possible distribution within the municipality. With concentration, integration becomes much more difficult. Of course, shelter must fit within legal frameworks and there must be great attention to safety in and around such centers.

statements.title: There should be more temporary housing in the municipality of Heumen.
Dropping off bulky waste at the recycling center should be free for the first 200 kilograms.

titleParts.glossary: Waste that is larger than an ordinary trash bin, such as old furniture.
A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

titleParts.text: moet voor de eerste 200 kilo gratis worden.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Residents of Heumen should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Heumen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
More business parks should be developed in Heumen.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the center of Malden.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: High-rise apartment buildings should be permitted in Malden.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality of Heumen should spend more money on events.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Heumen moet meer geld steken in

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: More camera surveillance should be installed in Heumen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: A women's shelter should be established in Heumen.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality of Heumen should accommodate asylum seekers at a single location instead of spreading them across the four villages.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Heumen doet er goed aan om

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

titleParts.text: op een locatie op te vangen in plaats van ze te spreiden over de vier dorpen
